description:
First subticket of the test runner arc. Parser sugar so that

  f(args) do
    <body>
  end

parses as Expr::Call(f, [<args>, <body-as-Block>]) — the do-block becomes a trailing positional argument. Caller-side macros pull it out with their last param.

## Scope

- Parser: in postfix-call (Tok::LParen branch in parse_bp), after consuming `)`, if peek is Tok::Do, consume `do` ... `end` block and append the resulting Expr to the call's args.
- Equivalent shorthand: `f(args), do: <expr>` -> append <expr> as trailing arg.
- Doesn't introduce paren-less calls (`test "name" do ... end` Elixir-style) — parens stay required for now.

## Tests

- Round-trip: `f(1, 2) do x end` parses identically to `f(1, 2, do_block_expr)`.
- The do-block can hold a multi-stmt body.
- Call without trailing do is unchanged.
title: do-block as trailing call arg
